title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity · Runtime Lens: Performance Regression
summary: A team optimizes string concatenation from one warm run, reports elapsed time without input size, never measures memory, and ships a faster tokenizer that changes word policy. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the performance regression increment in correctness-preserving capacity study. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.
Measure peak allocations or process memory for batch and stream paths against vocabulary and output growth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-performance-regression — Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-performance-profiling-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-performance-profiling-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_performance_profiling_2(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-performance-regression-1h2ml8" that uses case 4689 to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-performance-regression-1h2ml8
# Competency: bookbot-performance-regression.
# Case 4689: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_performance_regression_1h2ml8(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1h2ml8 = "1h2ml8-8lm2h1-4689"
    assert evidence_variant_1h2ml8
    evidence_axes_1h2ml8 = (
        "corpus1h2ml8",
        "policy8lm2h1",
        "revision4689",
        "command1h2ml84689",
        "report8lm2h14689",
        "test46891h2ml8",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1h2ml8)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"

Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Performance Regression model · Theory Performance Regression, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A faster result may be accepted even if Unicode or ranking output changes.
